description: Trigger discrimination for DETECTING edition drift + delta (this skill) vs PROPOSING the mapping edits (framework-mapping-refresher), broad citation-AGE staleness (source-currency-auditor), and owning the mapping content (multi-framework-crosswalk).

cases.prompt: Track which standard editions our skills cite, detect when a newer edition exists, and produce a delta of what changed.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — register + drift detection + delta, without proposing edits, is edition tracking.
cases.reason: Pinning cited editions and detecting drift with a delta report is this skill's core scope; it hands the delta onward.

cases.prompt: Given this edition delta, propose the specific updates to each affected skill's description, references, and coverage map.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse — sequential) — proposing the concrete per-site edits from a delta is the next step.
cases.reason: framework-mapping-refresher turns a delta into proposed edits for review. This skill only detects and reports the delta.

cases.prompt: Audit all our skills that cite external sources and flag any citation older than 18 months for re-verification.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — a broad age/staleness sweep across sources is source-currency-auditor; this tracks specific framework EDITIONS.
cases.reason: source-currency-auditor flags stale citations by age across all sources. This skill tracks specific standard editions and their deltas.

cases.prompt: Build the crosswalk mapping controls across ISO 27001, SOC 2, and NIST AI RMF.
cases.reason: multi-framework-crosswalk authors the mapping content. This skill tracks the currency of the editions such mappings cite; it doesn't write them.
